homeQuotes: Math is a superpower, Ani — let’s train it!
Numbers don’t bite… unless you divide by zero.
Every big idea in math starts with small steps.
Patterns are everywhere — arithmetic helps you see them.
Your brain is about to get stronger. Ready?
Math is a puzzle. Solving it feels awesome.
Even the hardest problems start with simple arithmetic.

chapters.title: Why Arithmetic Matters
chapters.introQuote: Arithmetic is the secret engine behind every math idea, Ani. Let’s open the hood and see how it runs!

questions.questionText: Which of these is the BEST reason why arithmetic is important?
choices: It helps you memorize long numbers.
It is the foundation for all higher math.
It makes you faster at typing.
It is only useful for shopping.
questions.hint: Think about what subjects depend on arithmetic.
questions.help: Algebra, geometry, and even physics rely on arithmetic rules.
questions.solution: Arithmetic is the foundation for all higher math. Without understanding operations, patterns, and number behavior, advanced topics become difficult.

questions.hint: How much does the number increase each time?

choices: Adding 1 always makes a number smaller.
Subtracting 5 always gives a positive number.
questions.hint: Think about the special behavior of zero.
questions.solution: Multiplying any number by 0 always gives 0. The other statements are not always true.

25 + 17 = 25 + (10 + 7) = 35 + 7 = 42? No — that was a trap. Correct calculation: 25 + 10 = 35, 35 + 7 = 42. But 42 is not in the correct position. The correct answer is 42 → choice index 2. (Your choices list must match this.)

questions.questionText: Which number is the odd one out? 4, 9, 16, 25, 30
questions.solution: 30 is not a perfect square. All others are squares of whole numbers.
8 + 8 + 8 = 24? No — check choices. Correct multiplication: 8 × 3 = 24 → choice index 3.

questions.help: Multiplying by a larger number gives a larger product.
questions.solution: 7 × 2 = 14, and 7 × 3 = 21 is correct reasoning. The others are incorrect.

questions.questionText: Which number is a multiple of 4?
questions.hint: A number is a multiple of 4 if it is 4 × something.
questions.solution: 28 = 4 × 7, so it is a multiple of 4.

choices: A system of patterns and rules.
A tool used only in school.
questions.solution: Arithmetic is a system of patterns and rules that help us understand numbers.
